solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public double minTime(int n, int k, int m, int[] time, double[] mul) {
        double[][] dp = new double[n + 1][1 << n];
        for (double[] row : dp) {
            Arrays.fill(row, Double.MAX_VALUE);
        }
        dp[0][0] = 0.0;

        for (int mask = 0; mask < (1 << n); mask++) {
            int left = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) left++;
            }
            if (left == 0) continue;

            for (int g = 0; g < n; g++) {
                if ((mask & (1 << g)) != 0) continue;
                int group = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                    if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0 && ((group >> i) & 1) == 0) {
                        group |= (1 << i);
                        if (Integer.bitCount(group) == k) break;
                    }
                }

                int groupSize = Integer.bitCount(group);
                if (groupSize == 0) continue;

                double maxTime = 0.0;
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                    if ((group & (1 << i)) != 0) {
                        maxTime = Math.max(maxTime, time[i]);
                    }
                }

                int stage = 0;
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
                    if ((mask & (1 << i)) == 0) {
                        stage = (stage + (int) Math.floor(maxTime * mul[stage % m])) % m;
                        break;
                    }
                }

                double tripTime = maxTime * mul[stage % m];
                int newStage = (stage + (int) Math.floor(tripTime)) % m;
                int newMask = mask | group;
                dp[newMask][newMask] = Math.min(dp[newMask][newMask], dp[mask][mask] + tripTime);

                if (groupSize < k) continue;

                for (int r = 0; r < n; r++) {
                    if ((newMask & (1 << r)) != 0) {
                        double returnTime = time[r] * mul[newStage % m];
                        int returnStage = (newStage + (int) Math.floor(returnTime)) % m;
                        int returnMask = newMask ^ (1 << r);
                        dp[returnMask][returnMask] = Math.min(dp[returnMask][returnMask], dp[newMask][newMask] + returnTime);
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        double res = Double.MAX_VALUE;
        for (int mask = 0; mask < (1 << n); mask++) {
            if (Integer.bitCount(mask) == n) {
                res = Math.min(res, dp[mask][mask]);
            }
        }

        return res == Double.MAX_VALUE ? -1.0 : res;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n * 2^n)
space complexity: O(n * 2^n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution uses dynamic programming to explore all possible subsets of people who have crossed the river. For each subset, it calculates the minimum time required to get that subset across, considering all possible groups of people that can be sent or returned. The key is to efficiently manage the state transitions and the stage advancement based on the trip and return times. The approach is efficient for small n (up to 12) as required by the constraints.
